title: SIGIL Provider Benchmark Matrix
thesis: SIGIL is not a universal compact prompt; it is a transport runtime calibrated per provider, model, and operating regime.
The most honest benchmark today is task-level selective extended routing with plain baseline allowed as a first-class candidate.
In that harder regime, Claude, Gemini, GPT-5.4, and now GPT-5.4-mini are all positive on aggregate total cost under the right routed policy.
The newest OpenAI lift comes from cross-provider task-contract transfer: `gemini-nano` is now a winning family on review, architecture, and part of debugging once the local runtime absorbs its drift.

A second new lever now compounds that: tighter per-lane output caps on already-winning families. `cap56` lanes improve OpenAI debug routing and Claude debug/refactor routing without changing the core grammar.

A third lever now compounds both: compiled shared context. On Gemini macro, compiled cacheable prefixes plus nano task contracts and low/zero thinking budgets beat both the older long-prefix steady-state row and the plain compiled-context baseline.

The strongest breakthrough rows still come from narrower calibrated regimes, but Anthropic is no longer limited to starter nano: the extended task-level matrix is now a clear win once transfer lanes and aggressive caps are allowed to compete across debug, review, architecture, and refactor.

A new top-tier holdout now confirms the pattern on newer Anthropic models too: `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-6` stay positive on total cost and latency with full parseability after local rerender.

entries.label: OpenAI GPT-5.4 micro breakthrough
entries.notes: The focused micro benchmark is still the cleanest OpenAI breakthrough row: full SIGIL wins end-to-end on total cost, output size, and latency.

entries.label: OpenAI GPT-5.4 extended task selective
entries.router: task-level multi-IR + transferred gemini-nano review/debug lanes + cap56 debug
entries.notes: OpenAI strong-tier is now more clearly positive on the harder extended matrix. The newest lift comes from a tighter `cap56` debug lane on top of task-level routing plus cross-provider task-contract transfer across review, part of debug, and part of architecture, while refactor still stays plain.

entries.label: OpenAI GPT-5.4-mini extended task selective
entries.router: task-level selective with transferred gemini-nano debug/review/architecture lanes + cap56 debug
entries.notes: OpenAI mini is now materially positive on the harder extended matrix. The newest lift is a tighter `cap56` debug lane on top of broader contract transfer: gemini-nano now wins on most review tasks, a large share of debugging, and part of architecture, while plain stays available where SIGIL is still not worth it.

entries.label: OpenAI GPT-5.4-mini macro compiled cold-start
entries.router: focused compiled context + transferred gemini-nano + cap56 debug + plain refactor
entries.notes: Compiled shared context generalizes to OpenAI too: the macro cold-start row stays positive on total cost while improving retention.

entries.label: Claude Sonnet 4 nano starter
entries.notes: Starter nano tasks remain Claude's strongest full-route result: materially lower total cost, much smaller visible output, and high parseability.

entries.label: Claude Sonnet 4 extended task selective
entries.router: task-level multi-IR selective + gemini-transfer debug/review/refactor lanes + cap56 debug/refactor
entries.notes: Claude is now even more strongly positive on the harder extended corpus. The newest lift comes from `cap56` on winning gemini-transfer lanes, especially in debug and refactor, on top of family transfer at scale across review and part of architecture.

entries.label: Claude Sonnet 4 macro compiled cold-start
entries.router: focused compiled context + gemini-transfer + capsule-mini architecture
entries.notes: Claude also benefits from compiled shared context: macro cold-start is positive on total cost, much smaller on visible output, and much faster.

entries.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6 top-tier holdout
entries.notes: Top-tier Anthropic holdout on a harder 8-task subset. After strengthening local repair for standalone `SIGIL:` lines and comparator drift, Sonnet 4.6 is positive on total cost, much smaller on visible output, and materially faster than baseline.

entries.label: Claude Opus 4.6 top-tier holdout
entries.notes: Top-tier Anthropic validation on the same harder holdout. Opus 4.6 confirms that the transport/runtime wins are not confined to Sonnet-tier models.

entries.label: Gemini 2.5 Flash extended task selective
entries.notes: Gemini stays positive on the harder corpus under task-level routing as well. The main effect of task routing here is better retention, not radically larger savings.

entries.label: Gemini 2.5 Flash macro compiled cold-start
entries.router: focused compiled context + nano contract + tb0 + selective routing
entries.notes: Compiled focused context removes the need for cache and still wins on total cost, retention, and latency.

entries.label: Gemini 2.5 Flash macro compiled + cache
entries.router: cacheable compiled context + nano contract + tb0 + selective routing
entries.notes: Best current Gemini macro row: compiled shared context beats the compiled plain baseline on raw and effective total, and beats the older long-prefix cache row on effective total.
